title: block: sftp.write
description: Raw shape of an `sftp.write` block. `connection` is kept as opaque JSON because every leaf accepts the shared `$ref`/`env:`/literal trio â€” that parse happens in [`crate::sftp::parse_connection_ref`].

name.description: Binding name. `$<name>` resolves to `crate::_rb_sftp::SftpWriteAck` for downstream blocks / `view`.

service.description: `services.<name>` of `kind: "sftp"` â€” mutually exclusive with `connection`. Exactly one is required.

connection.description: Inline declaration â€” leaves may bind from a prior block via `$ref`. Mutually exclusive with `service`.

path.description: Absolute destination path. Literal or `$ref`/`env:` form.

body.description: Reference to a bytes-shaped binding (typically from `csv.write`, `xlsx.write`, `pdf.render`, or `$input.body`).

mode.description: POSIX mode applied to the created file, e.g. `"0o640"`. Validated as `0o[0-7]{3,4}` at load time. Default `"0o644"`.

mkdir_parents.description: When `true`, missing parent directories are created with mode `0o755`. Default `false`.

on_conflict.description: Sub-block executed when `if_exists: "error"` and the target exists. Same semantics as `db.find_one.on_missing`.

IfExists.description: Conflict policy for an `sftp.write`. The three variants mirror the only sensible reactions to "target already exists" â€” overwrite (the default, idempotent), error out (so the handler can dispatch to an `on_conflict` sub-block or surface the canonical 409), or skip (no-op ack with `size: 0`).
